feed.title: SAP Community - PLM EAM/Plant Maintenance
feed.subtitle: PLM EAM/Plant Maintenance Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to extract and share custom fields from standard table related to order, notification and measur
entry.content: How to extract and share custom fields from standard table related to order, notification and measuring point?

entry.title: Impact of Not Settling a Maintenance Order in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I would like to ask for your guidance regarding Maintenance Orders in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

If costs or expenses have been posted to a Maintenance Order, but the order is not settled, what would be the impact on the system?

Specifically, if the order is processed up to Technical Completion (TECO), but Settlement and Business Completion (CLSD) are not performed, could this cause any issues or limitations?
For example:

  • Impact on Month-End or Year-End Closing

  • Open balances remaining on the Maintenance Order

  • Effects on Financial Reporting or Controlling

  • Any system checks or errors during period-end activities

I would appreciate any insights or best practices on whether settlement is mandatory before closing periods, and what potential risks may occur if settlement is not completed.

Thank you very much in advance for your support.

Best regards,
Jessada

entry.title: Plan maintenance list export til excel or CSV
entry.content:
Hello Community,

It is required to extract the PM for the 2025 and 2026 including work order implement, delayed or any backlogg. 

which T-code to use, any short and effective ways?

Thanks for your assistance

entry.title: Search help (F4) for the Person Responsible field is not working in IW21
entry.content: Hello everyone, The search help (F4) for the Person Responsible field is not working while creating a notification, even though the relevant HR master data is properly maintained. Could anyone know what might be causing this issue ?

entry.title: How can i use Framework Purchase Order (Blanket Order) in PM Module.
entry.content:
Below is my scenario.

User frequent creates small amount Maintenance orders with external services. They don't prefer to have individual PO's for each Maintenance Order. Since each PO needs approval from the Management. Instead, they want to create one PO (say for 10,000 Rs.) and then link the PM orders to this Framework order. Also, once the Framework PO approved by Management can be changed (due to release strategy).

how to generates purchase orders automatically?release generates PR,Me59N generates PO or other way(generates PO without PR?);

It's this a new PO or a new item for FM order

entry.title: POST Call for UI_MAINTWORKREQUESTOVW_V2 with C_MaintWorkRequestOverviewTP Entity gives Forbidden
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am trying to test POST call for UI_MAINTWORKREQUESTOVW_V2 with C_MaintWorkRequestOverviewTP and to_EventPrio Navigation for Priority creation but its giving Dump and Forbidden error on Gateway client but same works as expected on "Screen Maintenance Request" application.

We are having Maintenance notification created and when we Edit it and tries to add Priorities POST call works and gives created response in Network tab.
Same when I trying on Gateway client its giving Forbidden and Short dump below

Can someone help me to find what exactly I am doing wrong?

Thank you,

Ankita

entry.title: Clarification Needed: Functional Status of MRP Type V1 in ECC/S/4HANA Production
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am seeking clarification regarding the functional status and production usage of MRP Type V1 (Manual Reorder Point Planning with External Requirements) in current SAP ECC and/or S/4HANA environments.

We are evaluating consumption-based planning methods for externally procured operating supplies with the following characteristics:

  • Seasonal demand pattern

  • Primarily, break-in (reactive) maintenance consumption

  • No advance planned independent requirements

  • Variable replenishment lead time (observed 9–17 days)

  • Forecast model currently set to “N”

During internal review, it was suggested that MRP Type V1 may not be fully functional or widely supported in production systems. Since V1 remains selectable in the material master (MRP 1 view), we would like to confirm its current technical status.

Specific questions:

  1. Is MRP Type V1 fully supported and functional in active ECC and S/4HANA production environments?

  2. Are there configuration dependencies (MRP group settings, external requirements inclusion, planning file settings, etc.) that would cause V1 not to trigger as expected?

  3. Has V1 behavior changed in S/4HANA compared to ECC?

  4. Is V1 actively used in production by SAP customers for consumption-based planning?

Our objective is to verify whether V1 is a supported and operational planning method before proceeding with configuration changes.

Any clarification, OSS references, or real-world implementation insights would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Multiple PR's are getting generated from the Work Order but Collective PR indicator is enabled
entry.content:
Dear experts,

In the Work Order multiple PR's are getting generated for the Non-stock item category 'N' line items. The vendor & material group is also same for all the Non-stock line items. 

The configuration setting “Collective Purchase Requisition Indicator” is already enabled for the relevant plant and order type combination. Despite this, the system is not grouping the PR items and is instead generating separate PRs.

Has anyone faced a similar issue in ECC 6.0 EHP8 and been able to resolve it? Any guidance or pointers  would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks

entry.title: Status de sistema cambio en orden de mantenimiento a "Bloquear cerrado técnicamente"
entry.content:
Hola a todos

En ciertas ordenes de mantenimiento se activó el status del sistema "Bloquear cerrado técnicamente", hasta ahora no he podido encontrar que parámetro activo este status el cual no se puede reversar.

  • Los permisos de trabajo están cerrados.
  • No hay reservas de material pendiente.
  • No hay Normas de Liquidación pendientes.
  • El status de usuario está en "Listo para cerrar"

Espero puedan ayudarme con este tema.

entry.title: Asset Performance Management Flows and Architecture
entry.content: Please where can I find process steps for Asset Performance Management?

entry.title: Planned Depreciation Asset with value equal for Declining Balance
entry.content:
Hi All,

I need help, any idea to fulfill the requirement to make plan depreciation have equal value for each 12 period every year. Depreciation using decline balance with rate 25% for 5 Years and start depreciation 01.10.2025 ?

For start depreciation from 01.01.2025 has no issue, it already have equal value for 12 period in a year.

 

Thanks,

Best Regards,

 

Arogya K.

entry.title: Display of all business objects for maintenance orders or technical objects?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am looking for a way to retrieve all linked Business Objects or archived documents for a technical object or maintenance orders in a consolidated list.

Specifically, I am referring to the following Business Object types:

  • BUS2007 (Maintenance Order)
  • BUS0010 (Functional Location)

Using transaction OAOR, I can search by these object classes. However, the search is always limited to a single object instance.

Our requirement is to retrieve, for example:

  • maintenance documents
  • inspection reports
  • protocols
  • other documents created within maintenance orders

that were generated in the context of maintenance processing and linked either to the maintenance order or directly to the technical object.

Important clarification:
The solution should not be limited to a single maintenance order, but should instead search across all maintenance orders that are assigned to a specific technical object.

Example:
Functional Location “Pump Station X” is maintained regularly.
For each maintenance order, documents are created and attached via GOS / ArchiveLink.

Now, I would like to search for “Pump Station X” and receive a consolidated overview of all maintenance-related documents that were generated historically — regardless of which individual order they were created in.

My questions are:

  1. Is there any standard transaction or report that allows listing all linked documents for a technical object across all related maintenance orders?
  2. Can this be achieved via tables ?
  3. Is there an ArchiveLink or Content Server approach to generate such an object-based overview?
  4. Or would this require a custom development ?

Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: Perform Maintenance Jobs App
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are using S4 Hana Private Cloud 2023 & Fiori App . Attachment section of perform maintenance jobs are showing multiple object level to attach document . Our requirement is to default only Order object with document type as EAG  . Can any one please suggest how can we achieve this through standard SAP configuration ?

entry.title: can we use PP WC in equipment master data?
entry.content:
We have work centers created in PP module. While creating Equipment master, can we us the same work center in the Location tab? In the Org structure-Responsibilities, I use specific maintenance work center.

When settling Maintenance orders, where will the cost accrue?

What is the best practice followed?

entry.title: Service Order Determines Wrong Plant for Service Part (1010 instead of 1020) – SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am currently testing a Service Management scenario in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and encountered an issue when releasing a Service Order containing a Service Part item.

Scenario

  • Service Order created in Company Code 1020

  • The Service Plant determined in the service order is 1020

  • The service order contains a Service Part item (Generator Oil)

However, when attempting to release the service order, the system throws the following error:

Intercompany scenario is not supported for service parts.
The plant for the current service part item is assigned to company code 1010.

The confusing part is that the service order itself correctly determines plant 1020, but during validation the system still determines plant 1010 for the service part, which causes a cross-company posting (1010 → 1020) and prevents the order from being released.

Questions

  1. From where does SAP determine the plant used for service parts in a service order?

  2. Could this be coming from:

    • Product master plant data

    • Employee service location

    • Service team

    • Default plant determination

  3. What configuration ensures that service parts inherit the same plant as the service order (1020)?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: EAM_TASKLIST_CREATE – “Service package required” error when creating multiple task lists sequentiall
entry.content:
I am trying to create multiple maintenance task lists programmatically using EAM_TASKLIST_CREATE in ABAP. 

Scenario:

  • Two maint. plans are in a loop and for each i am executing EAM_TASKLIST_CREATE, POST and COMMIT.  
  • Creating two maintenance task lists sequentially in the same program run. 
  • Both contain service operations. 
  • Each plan works correctly when executed separately.

Problem:

  • When executed one after another in the same program, the second task list fails during POST with error:

" Operation ... requires a service package! ".  But this operation does not need a service package.

  • Debugging shows that in the method GET_SERVICE_PACKAGE , the variables are:

my_ext_header-packno = 0000397416    
lv_packno = 0000397313

This causes an early RETURN and service data is not filled.  my_ext_header-packno should be initial or the same like lv_packno for the service data to be filled. packno = 0000397416 is from the first plan.

What I have tried:

  • Clearing all internal tables (gt_opr_ins, gt_spack_lines, etc.) before second creation.

  • Executing BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT after the first creation.

  • Using DESTINATION 'NONE' in the function call.

  • Wrapping the call in a separate RFC-enabled wrapper.

  • Verified that gt_opr_ins-packno = 0000000000 for both plans.

  • Both plans work individually without errors.

Questions:

  • What is the recommended way to programmatically create multiple task lists with service operations in one run?

entry.title: How to add fields in IE05 Report
entry.content:
Dear All,

We need to add the standard field FGRU1 to transaction IE05. Please suggest how this requirement can be achieved.

Advance Thanks

entry.title: Proactive Cross Plant Planning in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi PM Community,

We are in a situation in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition;

Master Data maintained in Technical Object(Equipment)

Planning Plant 1001

Maintenance Work Center is 1003PM

Work Center Plant is 1003

When the Maintenance Item is being created we get this error;


If we change manually the Main Work Center Plant into 1003, then the error message get's fixed and item can be saved.

If we change the Planning Plant for technical object into 1003, then the Main Work Center Plant is 1003 in the Maintenance Item, so it fetches the Main Work Center Plant behind the Planning Plant.

In the configuration Planning Plant for Plant 1003, is 1003.

Is this a bug in system, is there something we should not maintain in the technical object master data or configuration issue?

What would be the solution that the data inside the maintenance item doesn't need to be performed manually?

entry.title: Scope of SAP pm module
entry.content: Hi sap community, i am new to SAP Plant maintenance module. I have made the following document with contents of SAP Plant maintenance module, can you please review this document and let me know if there is any room for improvementhttps://hclo365-my.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/personal/sukhman-singh_hcltech_com/Documents/sap%20pm.docx?d=w877a263e7bb44f759c0589dd326aee86&csf=1&web=1&e=mJMj53 .

entry.title: How to add a check for requisitioner for purchase requisition created from plant maintenance orders?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

We have a requirement to add a validation for the requisitioner field before creating Purchase requisition(s) from a Plant maintenance order. The Purchase requisition(s) could be created immediately from operation lines or external component lines while creating and saving the Plant maintenance order.

Requirement:

  • Restrict the saving of the PM order and the creation of the Purchase requisition if the validation fails with an error.
  • Return to the order creation/ change screen (IW31/ IW32) in case of error (error shouldn't terminate current session)
  • Allow the data in the PM order to be edited (error shouldn't block editing)
  • We need an enhancement that has all the necessary inputs for operation lines and components and allow to restrict the commit for saving the PM order and creation of the purchase requisition

What we have checked so far:

  • I tried to check the WORKORDER_UPDATE BAdI using the method AT_SAVE, but this method lacks the information on the current operation lines and the components as inputs.
  • Then we tried adding the validating in BEFORE_UPDATE method of WORKORDER_UPDATE BAdI, but this method gets called after the AT_SAVE method, so it doesn't serve the purpose.

If anyone has any experience with handling such validation, please share your inputs. Thanks in advance!

Regards,
Aavil
